meta.title: Thumbs Up for Art and Music — Vocabulary Set 3
meta.subject: English Vocabulary
meta.description: Part 2 words focus: creative, imagination, figure out, amazing, concentrate, remember, teamwork, fun.

questions.question: What does 'creative' mean?
options: Able to think of new and original ideas
questions.explanation: 'Creative' means able to think of new, original ideas. A creative person sees things in fresh ways and comes up with unique ideas that others might not think of.

questions.question: 'Imagination' means the ability to think about things that are not in front of you or do not exist yet.
questions.explanation: Yes! 'Imagination' is the ability to form pictures or ideas in your mind — things that may not be real or that haven't happened yet. Art relies on imagination to create original work.

questions.question: This puzzle is hard! Let me _______ _______ how to solve it.
questions.hint: To find the answer / solve it
questions.explanation: 'Figure out' means to find a solution or understand something. 'I'll figure out how to solve it' means I'll work until I find the answer.

questions.question: What does 'amazing' mean?
questions.explanation: 'Amazing' means very impressive and wonderful — something that makes you say 'Wow!' Creative students in art class make 'amazing things' that impress everyone.

questions.question: Playing in a band teaches _______. Everyone must listen and work together.
questions.explanation: 'Teamwork' means cooperating as a group to reach a goal. In a band, every musician must work with others — good teamwork makes beautiful music.

questions.question: In music class, you need to _______ — you must focus and pay close attention to the notes.
questions.explanation: 'Concentrate' means to focus all attention on something. In music, you must concentrate — you cannot let your mind wander or you will miss the notes.

questions.question: 'Remember' means to keep something in your mind and be able to think of it again later.
questions.explanation: Yes! 'Remember' means to keep something in memory and recall it later. Music students must remember many notes, rhythms, and lyrics — a great memory workout!

questions.question: The painting is _______ — it is one of the most beautiful things I have ever seen!
questions.explanation: 'Amazing' is used to describe something very impressive. A painting that is the 'most beautiful you've ever seen' is definitely amazing — it fills you with wonder.

questions.question: Sort these Part 2 words by part of speech.
questions.explanation: 'Creative' and 'amazing' describe things (adjectives). 'Imagination' and 'teamwork' are things you can have (nouns). 'Concentrate' and 'remember' are actions you do (verbs).

questions.question: Art class uses your _______ — you have to picture new ideas in your mind to create original work.
questions.hint: The ability to picture things in your mind
questions.explanation: 'Imagination' is the ability to form images or ideas in your mind that are not physically present. Creating original art requires a vivid imagination.

questions.question: Arrange the words to make a sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: 'Music is fun.' Direct, simple, and positive — the text emphasizes that music is enjoyable. (Note: 'having' is not needed in the simplest form.)

questions.question: Which sentence uses BOTH 'creative' and 'imagination' correctly?
options: She creative her imagination every day.
A creative person uses imagination to come up with original ideas.
He concentrated his creative imagination teamwork.
questions.explanation: 'A creative person' (someone with original ideas) 'uses imagination' (their ability to picture things in the mind) 'to come up with original ideas.' Natural, correct usage of both words.

questions.question: 'Concentrate' and 'focus' have similar meanings.
questions.explanation: Yes! Both 'concentrate' and 'focus' mean to direct your attention closely on one thing. 'Concentrate on the notes' = 'Focus on the notes.' They are near-synonyms.

questions.question: Arrange the words to make a sentence about what music teaches:
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: 'Music teaches you to concentrate and remember.' These two skills — concentration and memory — are key benefits of music education mentioned in the text.

questions.question: The OPPOSITE of 'creative' is:
options: Unimaginative / unable to think of new ideas
questions.explanation: The opposite of 'creative' (full of original ideas) is 'unimaginative' (unable to think of fresh or new ideas). A creative person always thinks of new solutions; an unimaginative person sticks only to what is already known.
